architecture: MVVM layers: UI (Activity/Fragment) -> ViewModel -> UseCase/Interactor -> Repository -> data source.
Dependency injection with Hilt/Dagger; scope dependencies correctly.
Expose UI state from the ViewModel via LiveData (or RxJava); observe with lifecycle awareness.
Domain models separate from network DTOs and DB entities; map at the repository.
conventions: Async off the main thread (RxJava/Executors/WorkManager); never block the UI thread.
Prefer immutability: final fields, unmodifiable collections; avoid leaking mutable state.
Null-safety with androidx.annotation @NonNull/@Nullable; use Optional at boundaries; avoid returning null collections.
Handle errors explicitly (typed callbacks/Result wrapper); do not swallow exceptions.
UI strings in resources (strings.xml), not hardcoded.
lint: Run Checkstyle + Android Lint (and SpotBugs where configured); fix issues in changed scope.
No star imports; no unused imports/variables; brace all control statements.
The base config ships `checkstyle.xml` (via `sr apply-base`); respect its rules — fix the code, do not loosen the config to silence a finding.
Run unit tests (JUnit) for ViewModel/UseCase logic.
templates: scaffold-data: Retrofit API interface + DTO (null-annotated) + Repository interface/impl + mapper to domain model.
scaffold-screen: Activity/Fragment + ViewModel exposing LiveData<UiState> + navigation registration.
Mirror the module structure the app already uses; do not invent a new one.
design-system: Use Material Components (com.google.android.material); theme via styles.xml/themes.xml — never hardcode colors.
Size with dp/sp; reuse shared views/layouts before creating new ones.
Support light/dark theme; handle insets/safe areas.
Content descriptions on interactive elements for accessibility.
library-docs: Networking: Retrofit + OkHttp + Gson/Moshi — match the repo.
Async: RxJava or Coroutines-interop/Executors; Local DB: Room. DI: Hilt/Dagger.
UI: AndroidX + Navigation component; follow existing usage, don't mix patterns arbitrarily.
